data.title: Central U.S. Manufacturing Activity Increases at Faster Pace -- Kansas City Fed
data.description:
By Ed Frankl


Activity at factories in the central U.S. improved further this month, with expectations for future activity jumping as the Federal Reserve mulls lowering borrowing costs further.

The Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City said Thursday that its Tenth District Manufacturing Survey's composite index climbed to 6 this month from 4 in September, marking a fourth straight month of expansion after more than three years in contraction. Economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected a lower reading of 3.

The composite index is an average of indexes for production, new orders, employment, supplier-delivery time and raw-materials inventories.

"Regional factory activity continued to increase modestly in October, and firms expect additional growth in the next six months," said Cortney Cowley, assistant vice president at the Kansas Fed.

Expectations for future activity in particular accelerated. Production and shipments increased moderately, while both nondurable and nondurable manufacturing activity grew slightly this month, the survey said.

Meanwhile price growth for finished products and raw materials edged a little higher from last month, according to the Kansas Fed.

The manufacturing survey covers activity in the Tenth District, which includes Colorado, Oklahoma, Wyoming, Nebraska, Kansas, the northern half of New Mexico and the western third of Missouri.


Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1144ET

Honeywell 3Q Aerospace Sales Jump Ahead of Spin-Off -- Update
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton and Nicholas G. Miller


Honeywell International raised its full-year earnings outlook and recorded a jump in third-quarter sales for its aerospace segment as it moves ahead with plans to spin off the division.

In the third-quarter, sales for the industrial conglomerate rose 7% to $10.41 billion. Aerospace sales registered 12% organic growth driven by strength in the commercial aftermarket and defense and space.

Honeywell shares rose 7% to $221.11. The stock is still down 2.1% this year.

"Aero continues to do extremely well, and they're maintaining their momentum of continuing to increase their win rates and backlog," said Honeywell Chief Executive Vimal Kapur in the company's earnings call. Kapur added that better Aerospace growth is offsetting margin headwinds in the energy and sustainability solutions and industrial automation businesses.

Aerospace's performance is set to improve even further next year as tariff pressures on the divisions' margins this year ease with stronger pricing, Kapur said.

Honeywell is in the process of restructuring and spinning off several businesses, including its aerospace division. The company said it will announce leadership and headquarters for the independent aerospace company later this year and the spinoff will be completed in the second half of next year.

For the quarter ended Sept. 30, Honeywell posted a profit of $1.83 billion, or $2.86 a share, compared with $1.41 billion, or $2.16 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were $2.82, ahead of the $2.57 anticipated by analysts, according to FactSet.

Orders increased 22%, led by aerospace technologies, as well as energy and sustainability.

The Charlotte, N.C., company now expects 2025 adjusted earnings per share to be $10.60 to $10.70, up from $10.45 to $10.65. Full-year sales are projected to be $40.7 billion to $40.9 billion, compared with a prior range of $40.8 billion to $41.3 billion.

Guidance now includes the impact of the spinoff of its advanced material business into an independent company, which is scheduled to be completed by the end of October. The separation is expected to have a roughly $700 million impact on annual sales and put a 21-cent dent in adjusted earnings per share.

Related to the spinoffs, Honeywell is streamlining the company into three reporting categories at the start of 2026. It will remove aerospace technologies and replace its energy and sustainability solutions segment with a new category called process automation and technology.


Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1141ET

data.title: Premier energies to buy 51% equity in transcon ind
Recordati Industria Chimica e Farmaceutica S.p.A. Announces Appointment of Mike McClellan as Chief Financial Officer as of January 1, 2026
data.description:
Recordati Industria Chimica e Farmaceutica S.p.A. announced that Mike McClellan will join as its new Chief Financial Officer (CFO) as of January 1, 2026 and will be based in the Group's headquarters in Milan. Mike McClellan, an American national, is a seasoned CFO in the pharmaceutical industry with nearly 30 years of experience across various geographies. Most recently, he served as CFO of Almirall (2019-2025) where he played a pivotal role in delivering sustained growth and profitability, leading business development efforts, guiding key product launches and engaging with the investment community.

Prior to that, Mr. McClellan served at Teva as Group CFO (2017-2019) and CFO Global Specialty Medicines (2015-2017). He also had a significant career at Sanofi where he served as CFO of North America (2012- 2015), CFO of Europe (2012012) as well as in several country CFO roles.

BASF Announces Departure of Dr. Nina Schwab-Hautzinger as Head of Corporate Communications & Government Relations, Effective January 31, 2026
data.description:
BASF announced that Dr. Nina Schwab-Hautzinger, Head of Corporate Communications & Government Relations, will leave BASF effective January 31, 2026, to take on a new professional opportunity. The company will announce the successor in due course. Nina Schwab-Hautzinger has led BASF's Corporate Communications & Government Relations unit since 2020.

Prior to that, she held various international leadership positions at healthcare company Roche in Switzerland and Germany, as well as at a strategic communications consultancy in Singapore. She studied at the universities of Mannheim and Waterloo, Canada, and earned her doctorate from the University of Zurich.
